summary:
Agent believes major mechanism of transporting drugs across biological membranes is via simple passive diffusion due to its prevalence among small hydrophobic substances moving down their concentration gradient; other methods are less common unless specifically mentioned such as large molecule movement which relies more heavily upon active processes involving energy expenditure from ATP-driven systems. To summarize concisely:

Passive diffusion predominates for most drugs facilitating transfer based on solute size & polarity while maintaining low energetic requirements compared to active transports needed primarily when dealing with bulky cargoes needing specific carriers or ion channels driving them against natural tendencies necessitating additional metabolic input typically seen only under special circumstances not universally applicable beyond niche cases within pharmacology contexts discussed here today concerning fundamental principles governing majority behavior observed clinically speaking broadly
